directive.title: RBAC part 1: role field on users (schema + migration)

auditor.summary: The build adds the user role enum and column as specified, but has two CRITICAL issues: (1) the CREATE TYPE statement lacks IF NOT EXISTS, breaking idempotency; (2) the 'users' table defined in schema.ts does not exist in the ground truth database schema, meaning the migration would create a new table rather than alter an existing one. This fundamentally breaks the drizzle-kit compatibility requirement. Additionally, the schema.ts adds extra columns beyond the directive scope, and the audit table's RLS policy allows unrestricted inserts. The build needs significant rework to align with the actual database schema and ensure true idempotency.

top_issues.description: CREATE TYPE statement lacks IF NOT EXISTS guard. Re-running the migration will fail with 'type already exists' error, violating the idempotency requirement.

The 'users' table definition is added to schema.ts, but the ground truth schema only contains 'rfqs' and 'auditLogs' tables. There is no 'users' table in the existing database schema. The build spec assumes a users table exists, but it does not in the ground truth. This means drizzle-kit generate:pg would produce CREATE TABLE users, not ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN, making the migration semantically incompatible.

The 'users' table definition includes 'email', 'name', 'created_at', 'updated_at' columns that are not part of the directive scope. The directive only asks to add a 'role' column. Adding extra columns to the schema definition could cause unintended schema drift when using drizzle-kit.
